text:
Manny Pacquiao has hinted a rematch of his 2015 blockbuster with Floyd Mayweather Jr. could be on the cards next year.

Paralympic swimming medalist Ali Truwit is running the TCS New York City Marathon just two years after surviving a shark attack.

Olympic champion Ariarne Titmus has announced her retirement from elite swimming in a social media post that took Australian sports fans by surprise.

Hosting the 2024 Paris Olympics generated only moderate revenues for France, with an almost negligible impact on growth, court of auditors reported.

Skateboarding legend Tony Hawk auctions his original 1999 X Games “900” skateboard, plus many other skate culture treasures at Julien’s Auctions.

Noah Lyles matched Usain Bolt with his fourth 200m title at the world championships.

Muhammad Ali’s refusal to sign his Vietnam-era military draft card is now a piece of history going up for auction.

Olympic champion Sydney McLaughlin-Levrone ran the second-fastest women’s 400m time in history on her way to a gold medal at the world championships.

Mondo Duplantis set the men’s pole vault world record for the 14th time on his way to winning his third straight world championship.

Ricky Hatton, the former world boxing champion who rose to become one of the most popular fighters in the sport, has died. He was 46.

American sprinter Erriyon Knighton received a four-year ban in a doping case that rules him out of the 2028 Los Angeles Olympics.

The wrestling superstar and actor chugged the beer in a manner that would make locals proud, properly capping his final WWE visit to Ireland.

Finley Weldon, 18, spoke about gymnastics coach Sean Gardner less than a week after he was arrested on a child sexual exploitation charge.

Olympic star Ilona Maher’s mission at the Women’s Rugby World Cup is about more than just winning games — she wants to grow the sport.

The bout is expected to be the biggest test to date for Paul, a social media star with 28 million followers on Instagram and 21 million followers on YouTube.
title: Paris 2024 Summer Olympics and Paralympics – NBC4 Washington
description: Watch all the action from the Paris Olympic and Paralympic Games live on Peacock
